head: f89a8a7
root_cause: absorb_atom_file_via_digest_pipeline_v1 creates a per-run candidate copy of active compact memory under .runtime/file_atom_absorption/<run>/memory_candidate, publishes it to active memory, but does not remove the candidate directory after successful publish.
call_chain: operations/school/run_agent_school.ps1 Live mode adds -Absorb to exact count cycle
digest organ writes cells/index/manifest in memory_candidate and deletes raw normalized input

run_agent_school.ps1: line 026: Live exact cycle args add -Absorb
lines 181-182: staged raw source and raw source dependency are checked deleted
missing: no Remove-Item candidateMemoryRoot after successful publish
invoke_compact_semantic_digestion_organ_: line 187: CleanupRawSource removes only input raw source, not candidate memory root

manual_cleanup_repeat_risk: YES, for Live School with absorption, unless retention cleanup is added. Test mode without Absorb should not create these memory_candidate copies through the exact-count route.

recommended_fix.behavior: after successful publish and report write, remove .runtime/file_atom_absorption/<run>/memory_candidate by default, keep compact proof and optionally latest N failed/debug runs
recommended_fix.must_record: candidate_memory_root_removed, removed_bytes, active_memory_hash_after_publish, proof_path, no reports/proofs deleted
recommended_fix.validator_needed: validate no stale successful memory_candidate dirs remain outside active memory after absorption success
